mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks such as text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a local-first AI solution with decentralized inference capabilities.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the LocalAI services.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai-linux-amd64

sudo mv localai-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/localai

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster where LocalAI will be deployed. Adjust the parameters based on your requirements.

```bash

kind create cluster --name localai --image kindest/node:v1.30.0

```

> Create a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path --name=

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to the Kind cluster.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI to Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI to your Kubernetes cluster using the provided Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI using Helm.

steps.title: Verify LocalAI Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running successfully in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the LocalAI namespace to verify deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from the Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall the LocalAI Helm release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade LocalAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl uncordon <pod-name>

```

> Drain the LocalAI pods.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Uncordon the upgraded pods.
commands: kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and repository URL in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI service may not be running or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Ensure the LocalAI service is up and running with 'kubectl get services -n localai'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes is unable to pull the specified image from the registry.

**Fix:**
Verify your image name and check your network connectivity to the registry.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved performance for existing models.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI service running with the command 'kubectl get services -n localai' showing the service details.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up and the necessary tools installed.
